title: Cephalotaxus talonensis
taxonomy: Cephalotaxus talonensis is a species of coniferous tree within the plant family Taxaceae. The family Taxaceae, to which Cephalotaxus talonensis belongs, comprises a small group of conifers. It is a member of the genus Cephalotaxus, which consists of several species of yew trees. Cephalotaxus talonensis is native to the mountainous regions of Yunnan Province in southwestern China. The species is often referred to as the Yunnan cypress, reflecting its unique characteristics among other cypress species.
benefits: Cephalotaxus talonensis has been traditionally used in some cultures for its potential health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. constituents: Cephalotaxus talonensis, a species of yew, is reported to contain alkaloids as its active constituents.
